assertions.expectation: Recommends Stable or Extended release channel to minimize upgrade frequency
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends Extended channel with detailed explanation of why it provides maximum control over minor upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion to block disruptive upgrades while allowing security patches on the control plane
assertions.reason: Response explicitly shows the command '--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades' and explains this allows security patches while blocking disruptive upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends a narrow maintenance window (e.g., weekends only) so CP patches happen at predictable times
assertions.reason: Response configures maintenance window for Sundays only with 4-hour duration using FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SU

assertions.expectation: Suggests isolating training workloads on dedicated GPU node pools separate from serving/infrastructure pools
assertions.reason: Response creates separate 'h100-training-pool' with dedicated taints and 'inference-pool' with different configurations, explicitly explaining the separation strategy

assertions.expectation: Recommends disabling auto-upgrade on GPU training node pools and upgrading manually during scheduled gaps between training runs
assertions.reason: Response shows manual upgrade commands in 'Between Training Campaigns' section and uses Extended channel which doesn't auto-upgrade minor versions

assertions.expectation: Advises configuring training frameworks with checkpointing so jobs can resume after any disruption
assertions.reason: Response sets 'terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 3600' with comment '1 hour for checkpointing' in the training job template

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver version testing — always validate in staging before applying to production GPU pools
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GPU driver version testing or validation in staging environments

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPUDirect/RDMA networking has strict GKE version requirements — verify compatibility before upgrading
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Compact placement: Preserves RDMA topology' but does not specifically address GKE version compatibility requirements for GPUDirect/RDMA
